1.7: Address: Mortgage Department, Ecology Building Society, Ellis House, 7 Belton Road, Silsden, Keighley, West Yorkshire, BD20 0EE
1.11a: Refer to contact details in section 1.7
1.11b: Refer to contact details in section 1.7
1.14: The firm may not act if the person dealing with the transaction or their immediate family is the seller.
1.15: The firm may not act if the person dealing with the transaction or their immediate family is the borrower.
3.1.3: Notification of the seller's legal representative's details is not required.
3.1.4: Contact point for unrepresented sellers is the same as section 1.7
3.1.5: Acceptable identity documents include List A: Recognised Employer ID card with photograph and signature
Acceptable identity documents include List B: HMRC tax notification or Benefits Agency letter
3.2.3: Notification of the seller's legal representative's details is not required.
3.2.4: Contact point for unrepresented sellers is the same as section 1.7
4.1: The Society provides a copy of the valuation report to the conveyancer.
4.3: Contact point for incorrect valuation assumptions is the same as section 1.7
4.5a: Contact point for re-inspection requests is the same as section 1.7
4.5b: Certificates of title must be sent to the contact point in section 1.7
5.1.1: Contact point for properties owned by the seller for less than 6 months is the same as section 1.7
5.2.1: Transactions where the seller is not the registered owner are not acceptable.
Contact point for exceptions is the same as section 1.7
5.4.4: Environmental or contaminated land reports are not required.
5.4.5: Regulated (personal) searches are permitted if compliant with the Search Code monitored by the PCCB.
Search agencies must maintain adequate Professional Indemnity Insurance.
5.4.6: Search insurance is accepted if it provides full protection and the title is certified as good and marketable.
5.5.3a: Contact point for reporting unmet planning conditions is the same as section 1.7
5.5.3b: Original or copy of planning permission is required.
5.5.3c: Original or copy of building regulation consents is required.
5.5.3d: Certificates of lawful use or development/established use certificates are required.
5.5.4: Contact point for reporting restrictions affecting value or marketability is the same as section 1.7
5.7.1a: Lending on flying freeholds is permitted.
5.7.1b: Lending on freehold flats is not permitted.
5.7.1c: Contact point for reporting flying freehold titles is the same as section 1.7
5.8.1: Security involving buildings converted into up to four flats is acceptable if the borrower occupies one unit and owns the freehold (individually or with another flat owner), provided other flats are on long leases.
5.8.5: Security comprising one of two leasehold flats where freehold reversions are cross-owned by the leaseholders is acceptable.
5.9.1: Lending on commonhold title is permitted.
5.10.1: Contact point for reporting restrictions on use is the same as section 1.7
5.13.1: Contact point for second charges or non-standard purchase funds is the same as section 1.7
5.14.1: Leasehold properties must have a minimum of 50 years remaining at the end of the mortgage term.
5.14.9: Contact point for lease-related matters is the same as section 1.7
5.14.10: Contact point for service charge matters is the same as section 1.7
5.14.11: Unsatisfactory lease terms must be referred to the contact point in section 1.7
5.14.12: Original or copy ground rent and service charge receipts must be provided.
5.14.13: Receipted copy of notice or evidence of service is required.
5.14.15a: Contact point for absentee or insolvent landlords is the same as section 1.7
5.14.15b: Indemnity insurance is accepted for absentee or insolvent landlords.
5.15.2a: Contact point for management company issues is the same as section 1.7
5.15.2b: The management company share certificate must be provided.
5.15.2c: A signed blank stock transfer form must be provided.
5.15.2d: The management company's memorandum and articles of association must be provided.
5.16.2: Contact point if unable to certify a search entry does not relate to the property is the same as section 1.7
5.17.5a: The Power of Attorney document must be provided.
5.17.5b: A statutory declaration of non-revocation of power of attorney must be provided.
5.19.1: Contact point for affordable housing, shared equity, or shared ownership schemes is the same as section 1.7
5.20.1: Lending on normal terms is permitted if solar panels are purchased outright, subject to evidence of cost quotes.
A Lease of Rights for solar panels requires Society consent.
Evidence of the lease is mandatory for remortgages.
5.20.3: Consent must be obtained for any Lease of Rights regarding solar panel installation.
Remortgages require evidence of the existing solar panel lease.
5.20.4: Existing Green Deal Plans must be disclosed.
6.1.3: Contact point if the borrower does not proceed with the offer is the same as section 1.7
6.2.1: Contact point for property description discrepancies is the same as section 1.7
6.3.1: Contact point for purchase price issues is the same as section 1.7
6.4.5: Contact point if the conveyancer lacks control over purchase funds is the same as section 1.7
6.5.1: Contact point if vacant possession is not provided is the same as section 1.7
6.6.1: Contact point for buy-to-let or letting matters is the same as section 1.7
6.6.2: Contact point for missing letting details is the same as section 1.7
6.6.3: A counterpart or certified copy of the tenancy agreement is required.
6.6.4: Houses in multiple occupation (HMO) are not accepted as security unless under a Housing Cooperative or Housing Association scheme.
6.7.1: Architects' certificates are required at minimum; must be ARB registered with adequate PI cover.
NHBC, Premier Guarantee, and LABC are acceptable.
Local Authority Building Regulations Practical Completion Certificates or Chartered Building Surveyor verification may be accepted via referral to section 1.7
6.7.2: The warranty policy and certificate must be provided.
6.7.3: Assignments of building standards indemnity schemes must be provided.
6.7.4: The lender may proceed without a new home warranty scheme.
6.7.6: A professional consultant's certificate is required.
6.8.1: Contact point regarding unadopted roads or sewers without bonds is the same as section 1.7
6.9.1: Contact point for missing easements is the same as section 1.7
6.10.2: Retentions or instalments are released to the borrower unless the offer specifies otherwise.
6.11.1: Contact point for redevelopment or road proposals is the same as section 1.7
6.12.1: Contact point for pre-emption rights or resale restrictions is the same as section 1.7
6.13.1: Contact point for improvement or repair grants is the same as section 1.7
7.3: Consent is required from all occupants aged 17 or over, excluding children of the borrower in full-time education.
Consent is required from anyone with a financial interest not named on the mortgage.
Occupier’s consent in the mortgage deed must be signed in the presence of the solicitor.
7.4: Contact point for inaccuracies in disclosed information is the same as section 1.7
8.1: The conveyancer may not advise specified third parties.
9.1: Indemnity insurance policies must be provided to the lender.
9.2: Indemnity insurance must cover at least 110% of the mortgage advance.
10.2b: Minimum notice for advance is 5 days.
10.3: There are no standard deductions from the advance.
10.7: Delayed completion funds must be returned electronically within 3 days.
The Society must be notified of the transmission.
10.9: Contact point for delayed completion is the same as section 1.7
10.10: Advances may be held for a maximum of 3 days.
10.11: Interest may be charged at the mortgage rate if the return of funds is delayed.
12.3.1: Contact point for release of retentions or instalments is the same as section 1.7
14.1.4: Form CH2 application is not required.
14.1.5: Original mortgage deed and original title documents must be provided.
14.2.1: Title deeds and documents must be sent to the address in section 1.7
14.2.2: Required post-completion: Official copy of the register, original Mortgage Deed, and all title documents specified in instructions.
16.1.1: Contact point for title documents is the same as section 1.7
16.3.1: The lender does not have a standard form of transfer or deed of covenant.
16.3.2: Contact point for debt amount enquiries is the same as section 1.7
16.3.4: The transfer of equity must be sent to the lender.
16.3.7a: Contact point for execution of transfer of equity is the same as section 1.7

16.4.1: Contact point for consent to let applications is the same as section 1.7
16.4.2: A copy of the proposed tenancy agreement must be provided.
16.5.2: Contact point for confirming deeds/agreements do not affect security is the same as section 1.7
16.5.3a: Deeds of variation must be sent to the address in section 1.7
16.5.3b: Deeds of rectification must be sent to the address in section 1.7
16.5.3c: Deeds of easement must be sent to the address in section 1.7
16.5.3d: Option agreements must be sent to the address in section 1.7
17.1.1: Contact point for redemption statements is the same as section 1.7
17.2.1a: Discharge and repayment remittance must be sent to the address in section 1.7
